steps.title: Evaluate f at t = 0 to find the y-intercept
steps.content:
The y-intercept occurs when $t = 0$.
$$f(0) = 8000(0.65)^0 = 8000 \cdot 1 = 8000$$
The y-intercept is 8000.

steps.title: Interpret t = 0 in context and eliminate wrong choices
steps.content:
Since $t$ represents years since the end of 1998, when $t = 0$, we are at the end of 1998. The y-intercept of 8000 represents the estimated number of coupons sent at the end of 1998.

Choices A and B mention minimum—the y-intercept is an initial value, not a minimum. Choice C has the wrong value (1,428).

That matches choice D — D is correct.
